source_authority.primary_file_role: authoritative local source for data-source names, providers, links, coverage, key variables, access routes, and ETL descriptions
source_authority.template_reference_role: confirms the requested data-source table fields and ETL slide requirements, but does not override project-specific source facts in ppt/High-Five_20260507.pptx

mentor_feedback_items_supported: 3.1.1 explain SKT and the data source
3.3.4 clarify Danggeun data source and scope
preferred_source_order: owning analysis repo public outputs and codebooks
reporting_boundary: Use PPT-visible source facts and aggregate/public outputs only.
Do not include raw platform text, post IDs, private row-level files, credentials, or local settings.
Treat blank PPT cells as unknown rather than inferring missing format or access-method values.

ppt_notes: Slide 3 states that raw data were requested separately on 2026-02-11.
Slide 3 states that the data were obtained via official channels.

report_use: Section 3.2 HLM respondent-level outcome and controls

etl_notes: Slide 6 says each physical base-station site is counted once.
Slide 6 says records are matched to a common Seoul district code system.
Slide 6 says station counts are converted into population-adjusted density measures.

source_evidence.uncertainty: format and access-method cells are not visible in the PPT text extraction

etl_notes: Slide 7 says public Wi-Fi installations are converted into resident-adjusted density.

source_evidence.report_ready_source_note: SKT refers to SK Telecom, a major Korean mobile network operator. The PPT identifies the source as SKT Telecom Data and links it through the Korean Public Data Portal; the variables used here are mobile data use, online service days, and fee delinquency rate for Seoul in 2023-2024.
etl_notes: Slide 7 says neighborhood-level telecom data are aggregated to district level using daytime floating-population weights.
Slide 8 says telecom fee delinquency is used as the burden side of affordability.
Slide 8 says the burden indicator is reversed so higher final values mean better conditions.

source_evidence.dataset_name: Average Monthly Household Income by District
source_evidence.report_ready_source_note: Use income as a contextual resource-side indicator only; mentor feedback suggests the report should not overstate average income as a direct measure of whether residents can afford telecom services.

etl_notes: Slide 11 says deleted, blocked, and empty-text posts are removed before analysis.
Slide 11 says text, time, category, and location fields are standardized when available.
Slide 11 says UMC keyword dictionaries are used to identify potentially relevant posts.
Slide 17 says cleaned posts, screened candidates, scored posts, aggregates, posterior tables, and figures are saved separately.
privacy_boundary: Do not store or report raw post text in this component.
Do not store or report post IDs, writer identifiers, or post-level URLs.

report_ready_revision_notes.note: When explaining SKT, use the PPT wording as the source basis and add a parenthetical expansion of SKT as SK Telecom.
For affordability, frame average household income as the resource side and arrears/fee delinquency as the more direct burden-side indicator.
If a 5G base-station choropleth is added, base it on Cellular Base Station Data from Spectrum Resource Mgmt System and reuse the district spatial-join/population-adjusted density logic from the PPT.

When explaining removal rates, say they refer to posts removed from the analytic text sample because the body text was deleted, blocked, empty, or otherwise inaccessible in the crawl, not to moderation rates as a substantive platform behavior measure.
